tossups.question: This company hired mosaic artisans from King Hassan II of Morocco during a project that reused material from St. Louis’s abandoned Riverfront Square for the agricultural pavilion The Land. This company’s founder, who inspired Philip Glass’s opera The Perfect American, led “El Grupo” on a 1941 trip to South America to promote the Good Neighbor Policy. Nathalia Holt profiled this company’s “queens” Mary Blair and Ruthie Thompson, who worked with its Nine Old Men. Michael Graves designed this company’s Swan and Dolphin hotels and worked on its model town, Celebration. This company, which produced The Three Caballeros and Saludos Amigos, has been led by the CEOs Michael Eisner and Bob Iger, who acquired Lucasfilm and Marvel. For 10 points, what company built Epcot at its Orlando “World?”
tossups.answer: Disney [or The Walt Disney Company or Walt Disney Studios; accept Disneyland or Disney World or Walt Disney World Resort; accept Epcot before read]

packet.name: L - Haverford A, Texas A, UNC C, Virginia Tech A

tossups.answer_sanitized: Disney [or The Walt Disney Company or Walt Disney Studios; accept Disneyland or Disney World or Walt Disney World Resort; accept Epcot before read]

tossups.question: This city is home to the world’s first aluminum-clad building, which was also the first building in the Western hemisphere to use a heat pump system for heating and cooling. That building, designed by Pietro Belluschi [“bell-LOO-ski”], is the Equitable Building. People often bump into each other while trying to find their name inscribed on one of the 50,000 bricks used to pave a square in this city. A Raymond Kaskey statue of a woman with a (*) trident sits on a teal platform above the entrance to a building in this city known for its tiny windows. This city shares its name with a type of limestone used extensively in St. Paul’s Cathedral. For 10 points, a postmodernist building by Michael Graves is named after what city in Oregon?
tossups.question_sanitized: This city is home to the world's first aluminum-clad building, which was also the first building in the Western hemisphere to use a heat pump system for heating and cooling. That building, designed by Pietro Belluschi ["bell-LOO-ski"], is the Equitable Building. People often bump into each other while trying to find their name inscribed on one of the 50,000 bricks used to pave a square in this city. A Raymond Kaskey statue of a woman with a (*) trident sits on a teal platform above the entrance to a building in this city known for its tiny windows. This city shares its name with a type of limestone used extensively in St. Paul's Cathedral. For 10 points, a postmodernist building by Michael Graves is named after what city in Oregon?

tossups.question: A theorist who supports this movement used the example of Antoni Gaudi’s ‎Casa Batlló (“bull-YOH”) as being “multivalent,” one of his requirements for the creation of “cosmogenic architecture.” A building from this movement features a giant copper statue by Raymond Kaskey atop a pedestal extending out from the green podium that makes up its first two stories. “Radical eclecticism” advocate Charles Jencks describes this movement’s predecessor as dying when the “final coup de grace by dynamite” destroyed the Pruitt-Igoe Housing Project. A cube-shaped building from this movement has white stucco facades interrupted by a grid of square shaped windows and (*) dark trapezoidal wedges. Both Michael Graves’s Portland Building and a building with an open pediment called a “Chippendale” are part of this movement. For 10 points, Philip Johnson’s AT&T Building is part of what post-1960’s movement that deliberately juxtaposed Classical and contemporary architectural elements as a reaction to the rigidity and lack of ornamentation of the International Style?
tossups.question_sanitized: A theorist who supports this movement used the example of Antoni Gaudi's ‎Casa Batllo ("bull-YOH") as being "multivalent," one of his requirements for the creation of "cosmogenic architecture." A building from this movement features a giant copper statue by Raymond Kaskey atop a pedestal extending out from the green podium that makes up its first two stories. "Radical eclecticism" advocate Charles Jencks describes this movement's predecessor as dying when the "final coup de grace by dynamite" destroyed the Pruitt-Igoe Housing Project. A cube-shaped building from this movement has white stucco facades interrupted by a grid of square shaped windows and (*) dark trapezoidal wedges. Both Michael Graves's Portland Building and a building with an open pediment called a "Chippendale" are part of this movement. For 10 points, Philip Johnson's AT&T Building is part of what post-1960's movement that deliberately juxtaposed Classical and contemporary architectural elements as a reaction to the rigidity and lack of ornamentation of the International Style?

tossups.question: To please this person, a statue of a triton copied from a Gian Lorenzo Bernini fountain was made with its mouth curving upward so that it would “smile.” In a structure named for this person, Manuel Rosales helped construct an organ nicknamed “Hurricane Mama” that resembles French fries. Michael Graves designed a pair of sister hotels named for this person that feature statues of a (*) swan and a dolphin respectively. A “magic wand” was added to a sphere featured at an “experimental prototype community of tomorrow” envisioned by this man. Curved stainless steel panels compose a structure named for this person that houses performances of the Los Angeles Philharmonic. For 10 points, identify this namesake of a Frank Gehry-designed concert hall and a Florida theme park called his “world.”
tossups.answer: Walt Disney [prompt on Disney; accept Walt Disney Concert Hall or Walt Disney World Swan or Walt Disney World Dolphin] (The “community of tomorrow” was realized as the Epcot theme park.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Walt Disney [prompt on Disney; accept Walt Disney Concert Hall or Walt Disney World Swan or Walt Disney World Dolphin] (The "community of tomorrow" was realized as the Epcot theme park.)

tossups.question: The Municipal Services Building in this city, designed by Michael Graves, is a major work of postmodern architecture. This city is home to seven bridges, two of which are the Burnside and Hawthorne, and it partially lies on an extinct volcanic zone known as the Boring Lava Field. Deschutes Brewery is found in this city, whose suburb of Beaverton is home to the headquarters of the Nike Corporation. It’s not Austin, but a popular slogan advocates “Keep[ing]” this city “Weird.” For 10 points, name this city on the Willamette River, the largest in Oregon.
tossups.question_sanitized: The Municipal Services Building in this city, designed by Michael Graves, is a major work of postmodern architecture. This city is home to seven bridges, two of which are the Burnside and Hawthorne, and it partially lies on an extinct volcanic zone known as the Boring Lava Field. Deschutes Brewery is found in this city, whose suburb of Beaverton is home to the headquarters of the Nike Corporation. It's not Austin, but a popular slogan advocates "Keep[ing]" this city "Weird." For 10 points, name this city on the Willamette River, the largest in Oregon.

tossups.question: A hotel in this state has a grand staircase leading only to a tiny coat check room, allowing guests to make a grand entrance into its lobby. Two post-modern hotels in this state are named for their massive rooftop animal sculptures and are Michael Graves’ Swan and Dolphin. A group of buildings known as “Child of the Sun” were designed by Frank Lloyd Wright for a college in this state. This home to the garish hotels of Morris Lapidus is the site of a lavish mansion called Ca’d’Zan, which was built to resemble the Doge’s Palace by circus man John Ringling. Philip Johnson used dozens of white columns for his brick town hall in the town of (*) Celebration in this state. Perhaps the most famous building in this state is a castle inspired by Neuschwanstein which uses extreme forced perspective to appear bigger. For 10 points, name this state where Cinderella Castle can be found in Disney World.
tossups.question_sanitized: A hotel in this state has a grand staircase leading only to a tiny coat check room, allowing guests to make a grand entrance into its lobby. Two post-modern hotels in this state are named for their massive rooftop animal sculptures and are Michael Graves' Swan and Dolphin. A group of buildings known as "Child of the Sun" were designed by Frank Lloyd Wright for a college in this state. This home to the garish hotels of Morris Lapidus is the site of a lavish mansion called Ca'd'Zan, which was built to resemble the Doge's Palace by circus man John Ringling. Philip Johnson used dozens of white columns for his brick town hall in the town of (*) Celebration in this state. Perhaps the most famous building in this state is a castle inspired by Neuschwanstein which uses extreme forced perspective to appear bigger. For 10 points, name this state where Cinderella Castle can be found in Disney World.

tossups.question: One art museum in this city was given an aluminum mesh façade in a 2005 expansion by Herzog & de Meuron. Kenzo Tange’s only completed project in the U.S. was an expansion of another art museum in this city that was itself expanded by the 2001 addition of a Michael Graves-designed Children’s Theatre Company. Frank Lloyd Wright’s Willey House is found in this city, which is home to an orchestra that ended a three-year lockout of its musicians in 2014. A Jean Nouvel-designed theater in this city includes a cantilevered lobby that juts out into the sky called the Endless Bridge. One of this city's tallest skyscrapers, which contains thirty-two corner offices on each floor because of a unique series of setbacks termed “zogs,” also has a seven-story Crystal Court. This site of the Walker Art Center and the Guthrie Theater is also home to Claes Oldenburg’s sculpture of a cherry resting on a spoon. For 10 points, name this Midwestern city that contains Philip Johnson’s IDS Tower.
tossups.question_sanitized: One art museum in this city was given an aluminum mesh facade in a 2005 expansion by Herzog & de Meuron. Kenzo Tange's only completed project in the U.S. was an expansion of another art museum in this city that was itself expanded by the 2001 addition of a Michael Graves-designed Children's Theatre Company. Frank Lloyd Wright's Willey House is found in this city, which is home to an orchestra that ended a three-year lockout of its musicians in 2014. A Jean Nouvel-designed theater in this city includes a cantilevered lobby that juts out into the sky called the Endless Bridge. One of this city's tallest skyscrapers, which contains thirty-two corner offices on each floor because of a unique series of setbacks termed "zogs," also has a seven-story Crystal Court. This site of the Walker Art Center and the Guthrie Theater is also home to Claes Oldenburg's sculpture of a cherry resting on a spoon. For 10 points, name this Midwestern city that contains Philip Johnson's IDS Tower.

bonuses.leadin: This design element folds entirely away to become a tabletop in Luigi Massoni’s “Dilly Dally” vanity design. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This design element folds entirely away to become a tabletop in Luigi Massoni's "Dilly Dally" vanity design. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these decorative objects. The “Ultrafragola” one of these objects was designed by Ettore Sottsass, and experienced a resurgence of popularity because its curvy, pink-lighted frame looks good in outfit selfies.

Sottsass founded this design group in Milan in 1980, known for its bright colors, terrazzo textures, and postmodern aesthetic. A style called “Corporate” this became popular in big-tech graphics in the mid-2010s.
Michael Graves was part of the Memphis Group, but he’s better known for designing the Swan and Dolphin hotels for this company, which also created a New Urbanist community called Celebration.
parts_sanitized: Name these decorative objects. The "Ultrafragola" one of these objects was designed by Ettore Sottsass, and experienced a resurgence of popularity because its curvy, pink-lighted frame looks good in outfit selfies.

answers_sanitized: Memphis Group [or Memphis Milano; accept Corporate Memphis]
The Walt Disney Company [accept Walt Disney World]

bonuses.leadin: Frederic Jameson helped popularize this term, often described in terms of the rejection of grand narratives, with a work that dubbed it The Cultural Logic of Late Capitalism. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this term that also denotes an architectural movement that emphasizes eclecticism and decoration, repudiating the earlier International Style, which an architect summarized with the dictum “less is a bore.”

The originator of said dictum, Robert Venturi, co-wrote this 1972 postmodernist book with Denise Scott Brown and Steven Izenour, reflecting on the exuberant and anachronistic buildings of the title American city.

Following disagreements in the 1970s, these two colors were used to name the camps of postmodern architects who either followed Learning from Las Vegas’s principles or, like the New York Five architects Richard Meier and Michael Graves, dissented.
answers: A Significance for A&P Parking Lots, or Learning from Las Vegas
the greys AND the whites [prompt on partial answers by asking “who were opposed by what other group?”]
answers_sanitized: A Significance for A&P Parking Lots, or Learning from Las Vegas
the greys AND the whites [prompt on partial answers by asking "who were opposed by what other group?"]

Following disagreements in the 1970s, these two colors were used to name the camps of postmodern architects who either followed Learning from Las Vegas's principles or, like the New York Five architects Richard Meier and Michael Graves, dissented.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about postmodern architects’ use of verbal and architectural quotations, for 10 points each.
parts: Robert Venturi stated this four-word quip in the book Complexity and Contradiction in Architecture. This phrase plays off another architect’s maxim extolling minimalism.

Philip Johnson’s design for 550 Madison Avenue has an ornamented open pediment that has been compared to the work of Thomas Chippendale, an 18th-century English artisan who made this general class of objects.

Michael Graves’s controversial design for the Portland Building incorporates many elements from this geometrical style of architecture popular in the 1920s, which was used in skyscrapers like the Chrysler Building.
answers: “less is a bore” (The other maxim is “less is more.”)
furniture [or cabinetry; accept chairs or any other specific piece of furniture]

parts: Name these places that also include the rustic looking Ahwahnee in Yosemite Valley.

Mauna Kea Beach Hotel was designed by Edward Charles Bassett of this triply eponymous firm, which is better known for their skyscrapers, such as the Willis Tower and the Burj Khalifa.

bonuses.leadin: This architect collaborated with Vlado Milunić on a Prague building whose twisting façades gave it the nickname “Fred and Ginger.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Postmodernist Canadian-American architect of the Dancing House whose other projects include the Walt Disney Concert Hall in Los Angeles.
Gehry‘s Weisman Art Museum is located in this American city, which is also the location of Cesar Pelli’s Wells Fargo Tower and Philip Johnson’s IDS Tower.

Michael Graves designed this other Postmodern building, named for the American city in which it is located. It features horizontal lines within a trapezoid at the top of its façade and vertical lines around its four sides which are themselves dotted by small windows.

bonuses.leadin: This architect designed a house with tubed white supports and large windows that bursts from a dense treeline. That house sits on a ridiculously steep slope in Michigan. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this architect whose trademark white exteriors also appear on Frankfurt’s Museum of Decorative Arts and the Sandra Day O’Connor Courthouse in Phoenix. He recently completed Tel Aviv’s imposing Rothschild Tower.

Meier is a member of this group of architects, which came to be seen as the major embodiment of modernism after a contentious MOMA meeting and study. It also includes Michael Graves and Peter Eisenman.
